data.title: Wall Street drops as jobless claims fall and Q2 GDP was revised higher
data.description:
Wall Street’s recent downward push continued on Thursday as the broader market averages observe selling pressure after jobless claims fell and GDP was revised higher in the third estimate.

Falling the hardest has been the tech focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) which has dropped 0.8%. At the same time, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) has found itself lower by 0.5% and the blue-chip Dow (DJI) is in the red by 0.1%. 

Across sectors, seven of the 11 S&P groups are in negative trading territory, with Communication Services suffering the most. At the other end, Energy has been the strongest performing segment on the session so far. 

U.S. Treasury yields are also on the move higher. The longer-end U.S. 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) pushed up 3 basis points to 4.18% and the shorter-end U.S. 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) moved up 5 basis points to 3.65%. At the same time the dollar index (DXY) tagged a 3-week high.

On the economic front, initial jobless claims dropped for the second week.

Q2 GDP was revised higher to +3.8% in the third estimate, intensifying the rebound.

Durable goods orders surprised higher in August, while the U.S. international goods trade deficit narrowed more than expected in August.

“Thursday's upward GDP revision for second quarter confirmed that the economy grew at a healthy clip, even as tariff uncertainty reached fever pitch during the quarter. The U.S. economy is resilient and the strong GDP is another indication that we are not at risk of any kind of recession, even with slowing labor market growth,” Paul Stanley, chief investment officer, Granite Bay Wealth Management stated.

“Thursday's GDP strength likely doesn't change the Federal Reserve's expected path of rate cuts, since the data is backward looking and was already well-known. The Fed wants to lower rates to keep the labor market in check and is able to do so at this time since inflation has remained under control, even though it's still above the Fed's target,” Stanley added.

Furthermore, the Fed balance sheet will come in later in the day.

In terms of Fed speak, Chicago Fed President Austan Goolsbee, New York Fed President John Williams, Fed Governor Michael Barr and San Francisco Fed President Mary Daly, among others, are all slated to take the stage in different events.

data.title: Freeport-McMoRan extends slide as Grasberg accident sparks concerns over global supplies
data.description:
Freeport-McMoRan (NYSE:FCX) shares extend losses in early trading Thursday, -5.2% to a five-month low, after the top copper miner declared force majeure on contracted supplies from its Grasberg mine in Indonesia following a fatal mudslide accident.

Freeport (NYSE:FCX) was the S&P 500's worst performer in the previous session, sinking nearly 17% in its largest single-day decline since March 18, 2020, according to data from Dow Jones.

Comex copper (HG1:COM) for September delivery -0.9% to $4.716/lb in early trading after surging 3.6% on Wednesday.

Analysts say a prolonged disruption at Grasberg - the world’s second-largest copper mine, accounting for ~3% of global production - could drive copper prices even higher, while intensifying supply challenges for smelters already facing feedstock shortages.

"This latest event highlights the copper market’s sensitivity to supply disruptions, [and] raises concerns about future growth in supply," ANZ analysts said in a note.

The percentage of copper production hit by unplanned disruptions has increased steadily from just under 5% in 2014 to 5.7% in 2024, and is expected to surpass 6% this year, and "over the longer term, this will constrain supply growth, creating ongoing tightness in the copper market," ANZ said.

Goldman Sachs cut its global copper mine supply forecast for 2025 and 2026, now estimating a total loss of 525,000 metric tons of copper mine supply as a result of the Grasberg disruption, lowering its global mine supply forecast by 160,000 tons for H2 2025 and by 200,000 tons for full-year 2026, and seeing Grasberg's production declining by 250,000-260,000 tons in 2025 and by 270,000 tons in 2026.

The revisions pushed Goldman's 2025 global copper balance from a small surplus of 105,000 tons into a small deficit of 55,000 tons.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:CPER), (NYSEARCA:COPX), (OTC:JJCTF)

data.title: Jabil falls despite Q4 revenue boosted by AI demand; auto sector potentially impacted by 'regulatory shifts'
data.description:
Shares of Jabil (NYSE:JBL) fell about 10% on Thursday despite fiscal fourth quarter results and outlook surpassing estimates.

The electronics manufacturing services provider's non-GAAP EPS jumped about 43% year-over-year to $3.29 versus consensus of $2.95. 

“Strength in AI-driven demand across capital equipment, data centers, and networking, combined with deliberate portfolio actions in Connected Living & Digital Commerce, more than offset pressures in Automotive and Renewables, highlighting the resilience of our diversified business model," said JBL's CEO Mike Dastoor.

For the fourth quarter ended Aug. 31, revenue grew around 18.5% year-over-year to $8.25B, compared to the consensus estimate of $7.67B.

About the Automotive and Transport sector, Steve Borges, executive vice president, Regulated Industries, at Jabil, said on the earnings call that, "this industry is emerging from a global market correction, while near-term growth of battery electric vehicles is slowed, long term outlook remains strong."

Borges added that "regulatory shifts, changing incentives, and trade pressures, particularly around Chinese EVs and U.S. tariffs, are reshaping OEM strategies."

Matt Crowley, executive vice president, Intelligent Infrastructure at Jabil, said that "what differentiates Jabil, is that we are positioned across the entire AI hardware ecosystem. Not just a single piece of the puzzle but the combination of compute, storage, networking, power, cooling, and the tools that make all of this possible." 

"Our strategy is to engage at the rack scale level. Instead of supplying individual parts, we are integrating full racks into one deliverable system, Crowley added.

Outlook

Jabil expects fiscal first quarter net revenue to be between $7.7B and $8.3B (midpoint at $8B) versus consensus of $7.50B.

“Jabil is well positioned to deliver sustainable value creation over the long term, with significant opportunities ahead in areas such as AI data center infrastructure, healthcare, and advanced warehouse and retail automation. We remain focused on deploying capital in ways that strengthen our capabilities and enhance shareholder returns,” Dastoor noted.

The company expects non-GAAP core diluted EPS to be in the range of $2.47 to $2.87 (midpoint at $2.67, versus consensus of $2.40.

For the fiscal year 2026, Jabil expects net revenue to be about $31.3B compared to consensus of $30.98B.

The company noted on the call that for the first quarter, Regulated Industries revenue is expected to be $3.05B, Intelligent Infrastructure revenue is anticipated to be $3.67B driven by AI-related growth.

Revenue from Connected Living & Digital Commerce revenue is expected to be $1.29B.

The company anticipates non-GAAP core EPS of about $11 versus consensus of $10.84.

Update: The story was updated with executive commentary from the company's earnings call.

data.title: TD Synnex slides even as Q4 results, guidance top estimates
data.description:
TD Synnex (NYSE:SNX) shares slipped 2.5% in early trading on Thursday, even as the software company reported third-quarter results and guidance that topped estimates.

Looking to the fourth-quarter, TD Synnex said it expects gross billings to be in the range of $23B to $24B, up 11% year-over-year at the midpoint. Net revenue for the period is expected to be between $16.5B and $17.3B, with the midpoint above the consensus of $15.99B.

Adjusted earnings are forecast to be between $3.45 and $3.95, with the midpoint above the $3.33 per share forecast. The company's adjusted tax rate is expected to be approximately 23%.

The strong guidance comes as TD Synnex also reported better-than-expected results, aided by its products and services. Adjusted earnings for the period came in at $3.58 per share, above the $3.05 per share estimate. Revenue rose 6.6% year-over-year to come in at $15.65B, above the $15.11B estimate. Gross billings for the period rose 12.1% year-over-year to $22.7B and were above the high-end of the company's outlook, TD Synnex said.

Free cash flow during the quarter was $214M and cash from operations was $246M.

“Our third quarter non-GAAP gross billings and diluted earnings per share established new records for our company” said TD Synnex CEO Patrick Zammit said. “Our performance is a clear result of our teams’ strong execution, a differentiated go-to-market strategy, and a global, end-to-end portfolio of products and services that is unrivaled.”

data.title: Existing home sales slip 0.2% in August, better than feared
data.description: U.S. existing home sales inched down 0.2% M/M to a seasonally adjusted annual rate of 4.00M in August, better than the 3.96M consensus and slightly below the prior month's 4.01M, according to data released by the National Association of Realtors on Thursday.

data.title: U.S. economy shows signs of productivity boom similar to the 1990s – KKR CIO
data.description:
KKR’s balance sheet CIO Henry McVeigh believes the latest U.S. economic data reveals a significant capital expenditure boom driven by AI investments, with productivity improvements reminiscent of the 1990s.

“AI capex actually grew more than all the growth in personal consumption,” McVeigh said during an interview with CNBC, highlighting the scale of technology investment occurring across the economy.

Despite manufacturing challenges, with the ISM index below 50 for 30 out of 32 months, McVeigh maintains a “glass half full” outlook on the U.S. economy.

He pointed to a productivity boom that “allows you to pay higher wages” and “endure the tariff hit to margins,” ultimately driving revenue growth for businesses in KKR’s portfolio of approximately 150–200 companies worldwide.

McVeigh indicated that while hiring may be slowing, there isn’t a concerning wave of layoffs occurring.

“Our businesses are doing fine. Maybe there’s a little bit slower hiring, but there’s no firing boom going on the way that you’ve seen that kind of predated past recessions,” he said, while noting that government and healthcare jobs have been major contributors to employment figures.

The KKR executive believes the Federal Reserve should continue cutting interest rates, citing weakness in the housing market and pressure on low-income consumers. “The housing market is very weak right now… About 21% of states actually have negative HPA home price appreciation right now,” he said, projecting two more cuts this year and three next year.

Looking globally, McVeigh described emerging “tripolar” trade patterns, with increasing regional commerce within North America, Europe, and Asia.

“Forty eight percent to 50% of trade in Asia is with itself. We think that’s going to go to 70%,” he said, creating opportunities in logistics and infrastructure.

He remains concerned about potential drops in AI-related capital returns and unexpected spikes in unemployment but maintains that the U.S. economy will grow at 1.5% to 2%, “maybe a little bit of upside based on the revision today.”

Initial jobless claims for the week ending Sept. 20 dropped by 14K to 218K, vs. 238K consensus and 232K the week prior. Also, U.S. GDP for the second quarter was revised higher to 3.8%.

data.title: Nvidia's dominion expands as Barclay's finds each GW of AI capacity costs $50-60B
data.description:
Proposed artificial intelligence capacity additions continue to mount, and Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) appears poised to benefit the most from the trillions of dollars being poured into these initiatives over the next few years, according to Barclays.

Barclays' methodology finds that each gigawatt of AI capacity requires $50B to $60B in total cost to build. They also calculate that a single gigawatt of capacity requires about 500,000 graphics processing units.

Earlier this week, Nvidia announced a plan to invest up to $100B in Microsoft (MSFT)-backed OpenAI to build and deploy at least 10 gigawatts of AI data centers with Nvidia systems. Using Barclay's formula, this one project could cost up to $600B and require 4M to 5M GPUs.

"With the wave of announcements that have come over the last 6-9 months, we now estimate over $2T of planned spend at ~40 GW of power in total," said Barclays analyst Tom O'Malley in an extensive investor note on Thursday. "Within that, we attribute ~65-70% to compute & networking with more deals likely in the pipeline, which starts to make the updated guidance of $3-4T look much more real."

"We see this as a positive for all accelerator names, Broadcom (AVGO) and AMD (AMD), but we see this largely flowing into the NVDA P&L over the next 5+ years, moving numbers materially higher and making this the most attractive name in our space," he added.

Barclays reiterated its Overweight rating on Nvidia and increased its price target by 20% to $240 from $200.

Of the 40 GW of AI capacity slated to come online globally over the next four to five years, some of the most substantial U.S. projects include Meta Platforms (META), 2 GW; Stargate, 10 GW with Oracle (ORCL), SoftBank (OTCPK:SFTBY)(OTCPK:SFTBF) and OpenAI; and multiple projects by CoreWeave (CRWV), Microsoft (MSFT), Amazon (AMZN) and Google (GOOG)(GOOGL) with capacities less than 1 GW. There are also several large-scale capacity additions planned for Saudi Arabia, South Korea, France, Abu Dhabi, Taiwan, Australia and the U.K.

In addition to hyperscalers and semiconductor firms benefitting from this buildout, several U.S. power and utility companies are poised to take advantage as well, according to Barclays. These include Entergy (ENT), WEC Energy Group (WEC), The Southern Company (SO), Evergy (EVRG), Ameren (AEE), NiSource (NI) and Alliant Energy (LNT). With these additions likely leading to higher energy prices, Barclays believes NRG Energy (NRG) and Talen Energy (TLN) are also preferred names.

data.title: Instacart expands Caper Carts features to track EBT SNAP eligible items
data.description:
• Maplebear (NASDAQ:CART), more commonly known as Instacart, has launched additional features for its AI-powered Caper Carts that give shoppers access to new ways to save and track items eligible for government food assistance benefits.
  • These updates on the Caper Carts smart screen offer real-time budgeting capabilities, and eligibility tracking for Electronic Benefits Transfer for Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program ((EBT SNAP)), along with loyalty program sign-up, improved product recommendations, and omnichannel savings.
  • Shoppers can also earn “Cart Cash” by clipping exclusive coupons and complete shopping milestones, such as making an in-store purchase three times in a month, redeeming Cart Cash directly at checkout.
  • With enhancements that include digital coupons and a personalized shopping experience, Instacart (NASDAQ:CART) has nearly tripled the number of stores with Caper Carts across 15 states.

data.title: Taylor Swift and AMC tie up again for promo film distribution ahead of her album release
data.description:
The release party film for Taylor Swift's upcoming album will be shown across 8,000 screens in 50 countries over three days as part of a promotional event.

The pop musician is collaborating with AMC Theatres Distribution and distributor Piece of Magic Entertainment to showcase her film "TAYLOR SWIFT | THE OFFICIAL RELEASE PARTY OF A SHOWGIRL" in Latin America, Europe, and Asia to promote her 12th studio album, "The Life of a Showgirl."

The musician and her team reportedly shelled out $15M for the second promotional project in which AMC is involved and bypassed the traditional Hollywood studio system.

AMC said in a statement Thursday it has struck an agreement with multiple movie theater operators, including ODEON Cinemas, Cineworld, Cinépolis, Cinemex, Cinemark, Vue Cinemas, UGC, CGV, Event Cinemas, HOYTS, Kinepolis, Pathé Cinema, Vue International, CineStar, Helios, Major Cineplex, Cineworld, Nordisk, and UCI, among many others.

The company said the film will be played in all 540 AMC locations in the U.S., will be shown in Cinemark (NYSE:CNK) and Regal chains, and will run in Cineplex in Canada. AMC has also partnered with Variance Films to bring the film to thousands of other movie theater screens throughout North America.

The musician's 89-minute film will run in cinemas from October 3 to October 5 only. Her 2023 collaboration with AMC for the "TAYLOR SWIFT | THE ERAS TOUR" concert film managed to rake in more than $261M at the global box office.

Swift on Wednesday also announced that Target (TGT) will be the exclusive retailer for a limited, one-time-only pressing of the vinyl version of her album.
